results.description: Silver grain is one row per soc_code. Duplicate SOC codes would cause fan-out in Gold JOINs and double-count observed exposure. The Silver transformer aggregates Bronze (task, SOC) rows to one row per

observed_exposure_pct is the SOC-level sum of split task shares, in percent units (0-100). It must be non-null and within [0, 100]. Values > 100 indicate the fan-out split was skipped (double-counting
After dropping the task_name='none' placeholder (1.78% of global share) and even-splitting multi-SOC tasks, SUM(observed_exposure_pct) across all Silver rows must equal 98.22 within +/- 0.5. A larger

Silver row count must be 500-650 SOCs. 588 observed in EDA against release_2025_03_27; band is ~+/- 10% to allow modest release drift. Count outside this range indicates SOC normalization collapsed or
Every SOC row must have task_count >= 1 (the aggregate groupby produces rows only when at least one task contributes). A 0 or NULL task_count means the grouping logic is broken.

At least 60% of Silver rows must have soc_match = true (SOC exists in consumable.occupation_profiles). Revised from spec's 80% per EDA finding (61.3% actual). Below 60% indicates either the source SOC

Silver soc_code must be normalized XX-XXXX. Unlike Bronze (which carries exactly one NULL soc_code for the 'none' placeholder), Silver must never have a NULL SOC because the 'none' row is filtered pre
Surrogate key record_id (computed via compute_grain_id with prefix 'aoe') must be non-null and unique. A duplicate or null indicates a grain-hash collision or derivation bug in promote_silver.

automation_pct, augmentation_pct, and filtered_pct (all 0-1 mode fractions) are volume-weighted means of the underlying 6-axis source. Their sum per SOC must equal 1.0 within +/- 0.02. A violation mea

results.description: automation_pct and augmentation_pct are 0-1 mode fractions (per EDA aggregation decision; despite the _pct suffix). Values outside [0, 1] indicate a unit-conversion bug (e.g. someone multiplied by 100

source_release is pinned to 'release_2025_03_27' per the release-selection logic (newer releases lack task-level aggregates). A different value indicates the ingestor auto-followed HEAD or picked a re
soc_title is required per Silver schema. A null or empty title means the SOC normalization lookup failed.

SOCs with observed_exposure_pct > 5 represent concentrated Claude adoption (> 5% of all classified traffic on a single occupation). Expected count is small (EDA: max 7.51; top-1 SOC 15-1199 at ~5-8%).
Informational: proportion of Silver rows backed by only one task. Expected ~33% per EDA. Tracked for drift monitoring; no threshold enforced.
